stage_one_pass_fail_basis: Stage One is treated as a pass/fail viability gate before Stage Two scoring; these checks are the local minimum review baseline.

stage_one_pass_fail_baseline.official_basis: Stage One is pass/fail; the project must clearly address the Agentic Ops challenge.
stage_one_pass_fail_baseline.remaining_gate: Public demo still needs approval before final submission.

stage_one_pass_fail_baseline.official_basis: Stage One should show credible use of Splunk data, Splunk AI, or Splunk MCP-style capability.
stage_one_pass_fail_baseline.remaining_gate: Live Splunk/MCP proof remains optional and approval-gated.

stage_one_pass_fail_baseline.official_basis: Stage One requires reviewable materials including code, public demo video, architecture, and runnable instructions.
stage_one_pass_fail_baseline.remaining_gate: Public repository URL and public demo video URL are still pending user approval.

stage_one_pass_fail_baseline.official_basis: Stage One should be reviewable without unsafe data, hidden credentials, or overstated capability claims.
stage_one_pass_fail_baseline.remaining_gate: Keep final submit blocked until public URLs and optional proof readbacks are approved.

mcp_bonus_claim_boundary.bonus_category: Best Use of Splunk MCP Server
mcp_bonus_claim_boundary.current_safe_claim: Designed for Splunk MCP Server, with local synthetic proof, SPL examples, local SPL-equivalent query evidence, and an approval-gated live proof path. Official Splunk MCP Server proof remains pending.
blocked_claims_until_verified: Splunk MCP Server generated the final submitted decisions
mcp_bonus_claim_boundary.upgrade_condition: Use verified-through-Splunk-MCP wording only after official Splunk MCP Server install/configuration, synthetic-only read-only MCP answer capture, safe readback, and reports/latest_splunk_mcp_proof_capture_manifest.json live_splunk_mcp_verified=true and official_splunk_mcp_server_verified=true.

criteria.title: Theme fit and required Splunk capability use
criteria.official_basis: The project should fit the Agentic Ops theme and reasonably apply Splunk AI, Splunk data, or MCP-style capabilities.
criteria.judge_message: Agentic Incident Command Center treats incident response as a Splunk-grounded evidence workflow, then uses an MCP Remediation Ledger to keep AI-proposed actions human-approved.

evidence.reason: States the Agentic Incident Command Center thesis and Splunk-native framing.
Shows the Splunk data flow, AI/MCP investigation path, and approval boundary.
Explains the intended Splunk MCP Server integration path.
Shows the live Splunk/MCP setup plan without executing it.
Shows the exact evidence-backed prompts and stop conditions for optional MCP proof.
Shows the capture slots and readback gates for optional live MCP proof.

criteria.remaining_gate: Live Splunk/MCP proof still requires explicit user approval.

criteria.title: Submission requirements
criteria.official_basis: The submission needs a public code URL, public demo video, description, track, architecture diagram, and runnable project materials.
criteria.judge_message: The local package contains the code, open-source license, architecture diagram, run instructions, demo script, public-candidate folder, and URL placeholders.

evidence.reason: Open-source license candidate for the public repository.
Copy/paste Devpost text with pending URL gates.
Under-three-minute demo structure and screen-safety guardrails.
Clean public repository publication evidence and stop conditions.
Shows repository/video URLs are pending until approved.

criteria.remaining_gate: Public repository URL and public demo video URL are still pending approval.

criteria.title: Technological Implementation
criteria.official_basis: Quality software development, consistent run behavior, and credible platform implementation.
criteria.judge_message: The implementation is deterministic, standard-library Python, testable locally, and produces Splunk-ready CSV, SPL-equivalent proof, a dashboard, and a packaged Splunk app candidate.

evidence.reason: Generates synthetic events, analysis, dashboard, SPL examples, and MCP preview.
Unit tests cover event generation and blocked/approval analysis.
Shows the SPL query intent returns concrete synthetic event evidence.
Shows .spl package members and package integrity.
Full local validator covering root, public candidate, scans, and smoke tests.

criteria.remaining_gate: Live Splunk import and MCP-assisted answer remain optional, post-approval proof.

criteria.title: Design
criteria.official_basis: User experience and design should be well thought out.
criteria.judge_message: The design is organized around the human reviewer workflow: identify risk, see evidence, decide approve/hold/block, and keep external actions gated.

evidence.reason: Main dashboard with KPIs, incident summary, approval queue, and MCP Remediation Ledger.
Static preview for README and Devpost review.
Recording-friendly walkthrough showing the intended user path.
Five-minute review path for judges and final reviewers.

criteria.remaining_gate: Public video recording still needs approval and screen-safety readback.

criteria.title: Potential Impact
criteria.official_basis: The project should show meaningful impact for observability, security operations, or developer productivity.
criteria.judge_message: The pattern can apply to any agentic workflow where AI can notify, publish, deploy, access tools, or prepare sensitive actions faster than people can review them.

evidence.reason: Frames the problem and practical value.
Maps built artifacts to requirements and impact claims.
Shows how external action gates can be audited and reused.
Defines completion evidence after public or submitted actions.

criteria.remaining_gate: Impact story is ready locally; public demo is still pending.

criteria.title: Quality of the Idea
criteria.official_basis: The project should be creative, unique, and clearly motivated.
criteria.judge_message: Most agent demos emphasize autonomy. This idea emphasizes operational control: Splunk becomes the evidence layer and MCP becomes a governed investigation interface.

evidence.reason: Maps the idea to judging criteria and bonus alignment.
Shows evidence-backed investigation output with event IDs.
Shows reusable query patterns for the AgentOps event model.
Defines how to upgrade claims after verified live proof.
Defines the judge-visible MCP prompt/readback path with citations.
Maps allowed public claims to evidence and avoid wording.

criteria.remaining_gate: Best-use-of-MCP claim should remain design/local-proof wording until live proof is approved.

criteria.title: Best Use of Splunk MCP Server
criteria.official_basis: Bonus prize fit depends on intelligent, agent-driven use of Splunk MCP Server for contextual insight and decisions.
criteria.judge_message: The package is built for Splunk MCP Server: an agent asks which operation needs human review first, and the answer cites event IDs, risk scores, policy decisions, and evidence references.

evidence.reason: Post-approval runbook for Splunk MCP setup and proof capture.
Command plan for synthetic import, app install, MCP setup, and proof.
Success criteria and stop conditions for live proof.
Prompt pack with SPL, expected event citations, success readbacks, and safety stop conditions.
Capture manifest with approved scope, evidence slots, readback expectations, and claim-upgrade gate.
Keeps MCP wording to designed-for/local-proof until live proof exists.
Checks that live Splunk/MCP claims are not overstated.

criteria.remaining_gate: Optional live Splunk/MCP proof requires explicit account/license/MCP approval.

recommended_judge_path: Review this scorecard by Stage One, Stage Two, and bonus alignment.
Keep live Splunk/MCP wording local-design-only until approved proof exists.
boundary: This scorecard is local judging support only. It does not publish, upload, connect to Splunk, configure MCP, write approved URLs, update Devpost, or submit anything.
